metric_value: 90% of Global 5000 businesses will exit 2006 with SOA planning, design, or programming activities underway
notes: From Aberdeen ESB and SOA Middleware study (June 2006). Cited here as supporting context. Very high adoption headline figure.

metric_value: ~50% of survey participants building SOA applications using web services and lightweight SOA tools (SOA Lite)
notes: From Aberdeen ESB and SOA Middleware study (June 2006). Kastner coins term 'SOA Lite' for this approach.

metric_value: Building new composite applications across custom-built and/or third-party applications using general-purpose SOA development tools
notes: From Aberdeen ESB and SOA Middleware study (June 2006).

metric_value: One-third of organizations planning four or more SOA projects in 2006
notes: From Aberdeen ESB and SOA Middleware study (June 2006). Indicates strong IT developer enthusiasm.

metric_value: SOA Lite projects may not sustain common IT failures or follow company or mandated policies due to absence of ESB, repository, management, governance, and lifecycle tools
notes: Kastner's critique of SOA Lite. SOA Lite applications may not be mission-critical-grade without enterprise middleware.

metric_value: IBM WebSphere suite of SOA infrastructure software meets the definition of Enterprise SOA
notes: Kastner endorsement of IBM WebSphere as Enterprise SOA exemplar: ESB, repository, management, governance, lifecycle development tools.

metric_value: IBM Software Group acquires Webify Solutions to add vertical-market SOA application building blocks to WebSphere and Rational portfolios
notes: Webify: 120-person private company; Austin TX and Mumbai India; pre-built customizable SOA components for insurance, healthcare, banking, telecom.

metric_value: General-purpose SOA toolkits (Eclipse IDE) lack vertical-market process orientation — forcing developers to invest time defining basic business-process service building blocks before adding value
notes: Kastner's 'bare bones' characterization of general-purpose IDEs for vertical SOA development.

metric_value: Webify provides pre-built customizable SOA parts, models, and business policies for insurance, healthcare, banking, and telecom markets
notes: All four verticals align with IBM's industry-oriented go-to-market strategy.

metric_value: Initial Webify solutions for IBM WebSphere and IBM Rational will cover insurance and healthcare; subsequent solutions for banking, telecom, and public sector under development
IBM with Webify is in a class of its own — vertical-market application components represent a viable adjunct to ERP systems from SAP/Oracle/Infor/Lawson
notes: Kastner positions IBM+Webify as complementing rather than replacing ERP systems. Competitive differentiation from pure horizontal SOA middleware vendors.

metric_value: Oracle process-model-driven Fusion applications expected next year (2007)
notes: Kastner prediction about Oracle Fusion release timeline. Approximately accurate — Oracle Fusion was announced but full deployment took longer than one year.

metric_value: Vitria's vertical applications may gain more consideration now that IBM is blessing the concept of flexible customization
notes: Kastner sees IBM move as validating Vitria's vertical SOA approach. Second-order competitive effect.

metric_value: HP has had outstanding vertical process models on the shelf for three years and now, with Mercury Interactive acquisition SOA infrastructure, is better positioned to enter next-gen application space — but has not yet done so
notes: Kastner criticism of HP inaction despite good assets. HP Mercury acquisition gives HP SOA infrastructure but HP has not entered vertical application market.

metric_value: Enterprise application programmer executives (especially healthcare and insurance) should evaluate IBM-Webify vs. Eclipse or Microsoft Visual Studio on metric of lifecycle lines of code written
notes: Kastner's recommended evaluation framework. Webify expected to allow programmers to do more quickly with fewer lines of code to maintain. Lower cost of application ownership.

metric_value: SOA services will contain a composite of packaged and homegrown applications for the foreseeable future — not a total rewrite
notes: Kastner quote from companion June 2006 Research Brief. Enterprises can continue to get value from existing application software investments while re-tooling processes as SOA services.

metric_value: IBM can take Webify technology and amplify it around the globe in products and services as only an $80 billion technology giant can
notes: Kastner's scale assessment. IBM's global services organization cited as amplifier for Webify technology.

metric_value: Webify enables IBM to hold business transformation discussions at boardroom level; time-to-market conversations with application programming managers; software lifecycle costs conversations with CIOs
notes: Three-level enterprise sales value proposition: strategic (boardroom), tactical (programming managers), financial (CIOs).
